zh.lifeSummary: 當William le Prouz Sheriff of Devon於大約1225年出生在英國戴文奧可哈普頓時，他的父親William Le Prouz12歲，母親Margaret Gidley18歲。他於1240年與Alice de Widworthy在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他在英國戴文被列為sheriff of devon。他去世於1270年10月21日在英國英格蘭戴文吉德利享年46歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭戴文盧斯特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William le Prouz Sheriff of Devon于大约1225年出生在英国戴文奧可哈普頓时，他的父亲William Le Prouz12岁，母亲Margaret Gidley18岁。他于1240年与Alice de Widworthy在英国英格兰戴文结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他在英国戴文被列为sheriff of devon。他去世于1270年10月21日在英国英格兰戴文吉德利享年46岁，埋葬在英国英格兰戴文盧斯特利。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Prouz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northumberland and Durham): nickname for a vain or haughtyman, from Middle English prod, prud ‘proud’ (late OldEnglish prūd, from the oblique form of Old Frenchproz).

variants.name: Proudman
meanings: English (northwest Midlands):
nickname from Middle English proud ‘proud, haughty, arrogant’ + man. Compare Proud , Proudfoot .
(of Norman origin): nickname from Old French prud'homme ‘honest, upright man’.

variants.name: Prout
meanings: English (mainly Cornwall): variant of   Proud  .
French: variant of   Provost   ‘provost’.

variants.name: Prude
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of   Proud  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When William le Prouz Sheriff of Devon was born about 1225, in Okehampton, Devon, England, his father, William Le Prouz, was 12 and his mother, Margaret Gidley, was 18. He married Alice de Widworthy in 1240, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. His occupation is listed as sheriff of devon in Devon, England. He died on 21 October 1270, in Gidleigh, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46, and was buried in Lustleigh, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William le Prouz Sheriff of Devon est né environ 1225, à Okehampton, Devon, Angleterre, son père, William Le Prouz, avait 12 ans et sa mère, Margaret Gidley, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Alice de Widworthy en 1240, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of devon à Devon, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 21 octobre 1270, à Gidleigh, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Lustleigh, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William le Prouz Sheriff of Devon um 1225 in Okehampton, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Le Prouz 12 und seine Mutter Margaret Gidley 18. Er hat 1240 in Devon, England, United Kingdom Alice de Widworthy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. sein Beruf ist als sheriff of devon in Devon, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 21. Oktober 1270 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Gidleigh, Devon, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Lustleigh, Devon, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William le Prouz Sheriff of Devon è nato il circa 1225 nel Okehampton, Devon, England, il suo padre, William Le Prouz, aveva 12 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Gidley, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Alice de Widworthy nel 1240, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. La sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of devon nel Devon, England. Morì il 21 ottobre 1270, nel Gidleigh, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Lustleigh, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William le Prouz Sheriff of Devonが約1225年にイギリスデボンオケハンプトンで生まれた時，父William Le Prouzは12歳，母Margaret Gidleyは18歳であった。1240年にイギリスイギリスデボンでAlice de Widworthyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。イギリスデボンでの職業はsheriff of devonと記録されている。彼は1270年10月21日にイギリスイギリスデボンギドレイグで46歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスデボンルストレイグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William le Prouz Sheriff of Devon(이)가 대략 1225년 영국 데폰 오케함프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Le Prouz(은)는 12세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Gidley(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1240년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Alice de Widworthy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 데폰에서 sheriff of devon이었다. 그는 1270년 10월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 지들리에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 루스틀리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William le Prouz Sheriff of Devon nasceu aproximadamente 1225, em Okehampton, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Le Prouz, tinha 12 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Gidley, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de Widworthy em 1240, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Sua profissão aparece como sheriff of devon em Devon, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 21 de outubro de 1270, em Gidleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Lustleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William le Prouz Sheriff of Devon родился около 1225 г. в местности окэамптон, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени William Le Prouz было 12 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Gidley, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de Widworthy в 1240 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына.  Он работал по профессии sheriff of devon в местности девон, Англия. Он умер 21 октября 1270 г. в местности гидлиг, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности лустлиг, девон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William le Prouz Sheriff of Devon nació aproximadamente 1225, en Okehampton, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, William Le Prouz, tenía 12 años y su mamá, Margaret Gidley, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Alice de Widworthy en 1240, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Su ocupación registrada era sheriff of devon en Devon, Inglaterra. Murió el 21 de octubre de 1270, en Gidleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultado en Lustleigh, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
